message.zeppelin_must_have.helm.detached: Airship Helm: no Sable sub-level detected. Assemble the zeppelin first.
message.zeppelin_must_have.helm.motion: Altitude: %s m | Speed: %s m/s | Vertical: %s m/s | Heading: %s° | Pitch: %s° | Roll: %s°
message.zeppelin_must_have.helm.physics: Mass: %s | Angular velocity: X %s, Y %s, Z %s rad/s
message.zeppelin_must_have.helm.aeronaut: Aeronautics: %s balloon(s) | Capacity: %s m³ | Filled: %s m³ (%s%%) | Lift: %s
message.zeppelin_must_have.burner.status: %s | Fuel: %s (%s) | Gas output: %s m³ | Envelope range: %s blocks
zeppelin_must_have.ponder.airship_helm_t: Reading an Airship with the Airship Helm
The Airship Helm identifies the Sable sub-level containing it
It reads position, heading, pitch, roll, linear velocity, angular velocity, and mass directly from Sable
The Helm also aggregates balloon capacity, gas fill, and lift from Create Aeronautics
Right-click with an empty hand to inspect the current flight telemetry
A Helm placed outside an assembled sub-level reports that it is detached
zeppelin_must_have.ponder.airship_burner: Airship Burners are fuel-powered lifting-gas providers for Create Aeronautics balloons
Insert furnace fuel, Blaze Burner fuel, or Blaze Cake into the burner
Redstone enables the flame and proportionally controls gas output and fuel consumption
The burner must point into a sealed airtight envelope within its operating range
The Forced-Draft Burner uses its data-pack profile to provide greater output and operating range
The Industrial Burner MK III is a configurable high-output model with a correspondingly higher fuel demand
Soul-fire materials switch the burner to its blue-flame appearance
zeppelin_must_have.ponder.tag.zeppelin_s: Control, lift, navigation, propulsion, protected redstone, and service equipment for Create Aeronautics zeppelins
message.zeppelin_must_have.burner.networ: Aeronautics heat network: %s active / %s connected source(s), combined output %s m³
zeppelin_must_have.ponder.airship_burner: Each burner remains an independent Aeronautics provider; the balloon combines every connected source in its native heater collection
Engineer's Goggles show both the selected burner reservoir and the combined output of all sources connected to the balloon
message.zeppelin_must_have.upgrade.missi: This module has no loaded server upgrade definition
message.zeppelin_must_have.upgrade.wrong: This upgrade is not compatible with an Airship Burner
message.zeppelin_must_have.upgrade.slot_: The required upgrade slot is already occupied
message.zeppelin_must_have.upgrade.confl: This module conflicts with an installed upgrade
zeppelin_must_have.ponder.airship_burner: Every Airship Burner has Thermal, Airflow, and Control upgrade sockets; each socket accepts one compatible data-driven module
The Heat Recuperator trades a little peak output for lower fuel use and a larger shared heat reserve
Forced Induction raises gas output and envelope range, but increases fuel demand
The Precision Regulator reshapes the redstone throttle curve for finer low-power control
Sneak-use the Create Wrench to remove the most recently occupied upgrade socket before dismantling the burner
message.zeppelin_must_have.piped_redston: Repeater delay: %s redstone tick(s) / %s game ticks
zeppelin_must_have.ponder.piped_redstone: Right-click the repeater to cycle through one to four redstone ticks, equal to two to eight game ticks
Mixed-MK networks use the weakest segment's delay and maximum distance, so upgrading the whole trunk matters
Piped Redstone carries the complete analog signal through sealed, waterloggable conduits
Connections are explicit ports, not automatic adjacency; parallel lines can pass beside each other without merging
Use the Create Wrench on a face to open or close that exact port
Waterlogging does not wash away the conduit or interrupt its signal
MK I, MK II, and MK III conduits progressively reduce network delay and increase repeater-free distance
A Piped Redstone Repeater preserves analog strength and starts a new distance segment after its own delay
With another conduit in hand, Create Placement Assist extends the far end of the line like a Shaft and preserves its axis
The Native Lever mounts directly to a conduit port, opens that port automatically, and powers only the attached isolated line
message.zeppelin_must_have.altitude_gaug: Altitude Gauge is not inside an assembled Sable sub-level
Altitude hold disabled; trim signal passes through unchanged
%s · altitude %s m · vertical %s m/s · trim %s → output %s
Altitude hold active: target %s m · error %s m
Altitude hold standby: target %s m · error %s m
zeppelin_must_have.goggles.altitude_gaug: Sneak + empty hand: capture or disable altitude hold
zeppelin_must_have.ponder.altitude_contr: The Altitude Gauge reads authoritative height, vertical speed, and balloon fill from the containing airship
Use the Create Wrench to cycle between altitude, vertical-speed, balloon-fill, and altitude-hold output modes
The rear face accepts a manual trim signal from a Native Lever or another analog redstone source
Sneak-right-click with an empty hand to capture the current altitude and arm the controller
The controller adds proportional altitude correction and vertical-speed damping to the trim signal
Route the front output through Piped Redstone to the airship burners for a closed-loop altitude hold
Signal slew limiting prevents abrupt burner changes and reduces oscillation
BoilerHeater directly below the bottom tank layer
Each MK variant is a complete Create Fluid Tank and forms native multiblocks with matching blocks
Create still calculates capacity, water input, boiler size, engines, efficiency, and Stress Units
MK I upgrades ordinary heater output while preserving passive heat as passive
MK II transfers more heat per heater column for compact high-power boilers
MK III is an industrial pressure vessel for maximum-output airship power plants
Different MK variants never merge; every multiblock must be built from one MK series
Goggles show native boiler data and the active MK profile; the Wrench toggles tank windows
block.zeppelin_must_have.brass_steam_eng: Brass Compound Steam Engine — MK II
block.zeppelin_must_have.industrial_stea: Industrial Triple-Expansion Steam Engine — MK III
zeppelin_must_have.ponder.ballast_tank.t: The Ballast Tank accepts water through buckets, pipes, and any NeoForge fluid handler
Stored water is registered as real dynamic mass in the containing Sable sub-level
Moving ballast changes both total vessel mass and its center of mass; a comparator reports the fill level
Mooring a Zeppelin with a Physical Rope
The Mooring Winch is a specialized Create Simulated Rope Winch, not a decorative cable block
Kinetic rotation extends or retracts the rope through the native winch behavior
The deployed line uses the Simulated rope strand, attachment, tension, break force, and physics constraints
zeppelin_must_have.ponder.vertical_thrus: Create kinetic speed drives the Vertical Thruster through its shaft connection
Aeronautics exposes the propeller and Sable applies the resulting force at the block position on every physics tick
Use the Create Wrench on the fan to reverse thrust or flip the unit between upward and downward operation
zeppelin_must_have.zeppelin_part.descrip: Server-authoritative flight telemetry for a moving Sable airship.
MK I fuel burner for heating Aeronautics lifting envelopes.
MK II burner with increased output and envelope range.
MK III burner for heavy-lift and large-envelope zeppelins.
Thermal module that extends burner endurance and heat capacity.
Airflow module that increases burner output and cast range.
Control module that improves low-signal throttle regulation.
MK I connected pressure vessel for compact steam systems.
MK II pressure vessel with increased heat conversion.
MK III pressure vessel for maximum steam generation.
Single-cylinder MK I Create-compatible steam engine.
Two-cylinder compound MK II steam engine.
Three-cylinder MK III triple-expansion steam engine.
Waterproof analog conduit with a 32-edge range.
Fast waterproof analog conduit with a 64-edge range.
Fastest waterproof analog conduit with a 128-edge range.
Conduit-mounted isolated lever for one protected signal line.
Waterproof analog repeater that resets range and adds delay.
Water ballast tank that changes real Sable mass and center of mass.
Kinetic physical-rope winch for anchoring simulated airships.
Flight sensor and closed-loop altitude-hold controller.
Create-powered vertical Aeronautics propeller with physical thrust.
Server-authoritative vessel computer that combines telemetry and routes independent flight-control channels.
Seven-position ahead/astern engine order control for the Flight Control Network.
Vessel-wide manual safety trip. Stops controllers and requires a manual reset.
Publishes a local analog signal to one named vessel channel.
Receives one vessel channel as analog redstone or a direct actuator command.
zeppelin_must_have.fcn.computer.flight: Altitude %s | vertical speed %s | heading %s | pitch %s | roll %s | speed %s
zeppelin_must_have.fcn.emergency.activat: EMERGENCY CUTOFF LATCHED. Burners, thrust, engines, and automatic controllers stopped.
zeppelin_must_have.fcn.emergency.reset: Emergency cutoff manually reset. Controllers must reacquire the network.
zeppelin_must_have.fcn.emergency.detache: Emergency cutoff is not mounted on a Sable vessel sub-level.
zeppelin_must_have.fcn.emergency.manual_: Emergency remains latched. Sneak-use the cutoff for a manual reset.
zeppelin_must_have.fcn.computer.structur: Mass %s | center of mass [%s, %s, %s] | envelope fill %s%% | lift %s
zeppelin_must_have.zeppelin_part.descrip: MK II airtight envelope with a reinforced frame and improved damage resistance.
MK III pressure-retaining envelope for large industrial airships.
MK II Create-compatible pipe; converts pump pressure into 1.5× effective throughput.
MK III high-pressure trunk; converts pump pressure into 2× effective throughput.
block.zeppelin_must_have.grand_steam_eng: Grand Four-Cylinder Steam Engine — MK IV
zeppelin_must_have.zeppelin_part.descrip: Flagship four-cylinder MK IV steam engine for the largest airship power systems.
advancement.zeppelin_must_have.engineeri: Craft a Flight Control Zeppelin Part.
Craft a lift or envelope Zeppelin Part.
Craft a boiler or graded Steam Engine.
Craft a reinforced airship fluid pipe.
Craft a protected Piped Redstone component.
Craft every Zeppelin Part in the catalog.
Activate a Zeppelin Must Have system.
Read live telemetry from a Helm attached to an airship.
Fuel and ignite an Airship Burner.
Install an upgrade module in an Airship Burner.
Capture a target altitude and engage Altitude Hold.
Configure a Flight Computer for vessel control.
Issue an engine order with the Engine Telegraph.
Configure a Control Transmitter or Receiver.
Load water into a Ballast Tank.
Energize an isolated Piped Redstone line.
Bring a graded Steam Engine under pressure.
Generate physical thrust with a Vertical Thruster.
Attach a physical mooring rope to the vessel.
zeppelin_must_have.ponder.flight_control: Routing Commands through the Flight Control Network
The Flight Computer combines vessel telemetry and publishes independent control channels
The Engine Telegraph sends manual ahead, stop, and astern orders
Transmitters and receivers share a network name and one of sixteen dye frequencies inside one vessel
Use the Create Wrench to select the command channel carried by a transmitter or receiver
The Emergency Cutoff latches a vessel-wide stop and requires a manual reset
zeppelin_must_have.ponder.fluid_pipe_gra: MK II Reinforced Fluid Pipes open along their axis and render as one continuous protected line
MK III Industrial Fluid Trunks use the same Create network behavior for high-throughput boiler and ballast systems
zeppelin_must_have.ponder.envelope_grade: Matching MK II Reinforced Envelope blocks join into one continuous hull panel; internal ribs disappear
MK III Industrial Envelope blocks use the same connected geometry with a cleaner pressure-hull finish
Only matching MK series merge; mixed envelope materials retain a visible structural joint
zeppelin_must_have.ponder.steam_engine_g: MK I uses one Create-style cylinder and provides the baseline 1024 SU capacity
MK II uses two narrower cylinders in 180-degree opposition for a smoother compound stroke
MK III uses three cylinders phased 120 degrees apart and a reinforced industrial housing
MK IV is the four-cylinder flagship engine for the largest boiler and propulsion installations
MK V uses five cylinders phased 72 degrees apart, a rotating pressure core, and 12,288 SU for capital-class airships.
MK VI uses eight cylinders in a four-cell T-frame: two lateral banks and one forward shaft nose; every auxiliary cell must be clear before placement.
block.zeppelin_must_have.sovereign_steam: Sovereign Five-Cylinder Steam Engine — MK V
zeppelin_must_have.zeppelin_part.descrip: Capital-class five-cylinder MK V steam engine with a rotating pressure core; generates 12,288 SU and consumes 6 boiler engine units.
zeppelin_must_have.ponder.steam_engine_g: Higher MK series provide more Stress Units but consume multiple boiler engine units, preserving heat and water limits
block.zeppelin_must_have.leviathan_steam: Leviathan T-Frame Steam Engine — MK VI
zeppelin_must_have.zeppelin_part.descrip: Eight-cylinder MK VI T-frame engine. Requires an Industrial Boiler MK III and three clear auxiliary cells; produces 20,480 SU at a 10-unit boiler load.
zeppelin_must_have.placement.leviathan.r: MK VI must be attached directly to an Industrial Boiler Base — MK III.
MK VI requires three free cells: left cylinder bank, right cylinder bank, and forward shaft nose.
zeppelin_must_have.ponder.steam_engine_g: Engineer's Goggles show capacity, boiler load, cylinder count, and the active data-pack profile
block.zeppelin_must_have.mk_vii_steam_en: Industrial Triple-Bank Steam Engine — MK VII
zeppelin_must_have.zeppelin_part.descrip: Three-block-wide MK VII engine with nine internal crankshafts, a central reduction gearbox, and one Create-compatible output shaft.
zeppelin_must_have.placement.mk_vii.requ: MK VII requires three adjacent Industrial Boiler MK III cells behind its full width.
MK VII requires two free side-body cells, a clear three-cell service row, and one free central output position.
